summary: McKenzie shares a brief revenue update for his software business Bingo Card Creator, showing that in just the first 45 days of 2007 he has already earned half of his entire 2006 revenue. He sets targets of $1,000/month by April and $10,000 for the full year, noting that profits are tracking similarly well against the prior year.
key_points: 2007 revenue in 45 days ($1,050) already equals half of all 2006 revenue ($2,100)
He sets concrete, escalating financial targets: $1,000/month by April and $10,000 for the year
He distinguishes revenue from profit, noting profits are a separate and harder matter
money_quotes: Sales for the entire year, 2006: $2,100 or so
Still got quite a bit to go if I'm going to make my $1,000 in April and $10,000 in 2007 targets but I think I can do it.
Profits are, of course, another matter.
I have this funny feeling I will do better than last year.
key_insight: Tracking and publicly sharing concrete revenue milestones creates accountability and makes growth tangible, even at very small scales.
